description: Fix two bugs in the publish-plugins.yml GitHub Actions workflow: (1) git diff crashes when github.event.before is an unreachable SHA after force-push, and (2) plugins are built with stale @opentabs-dev/plugin-tools from npm instead of the local monorepo version, so platform fixes (like icon generation bug fixes) don't take effect until a manual platform publish.

userStories.title: Handle unreachable before SHA in publish-plugins detection
userStories.description: Add a reachability check for the github.event.before SHA before using it in git diff. If the SHA is unreachable (force-push, history rewrite), fall back to HEAD~1.
acceptanceCriteria: The detect step validates the BEFORE SHA with `git cat-file -e` before passing it to `git diff`
If the SHA is unreachable (exit code non-zero), BEFORE falls back to HEAD~1
The existing zero-SHA check (all zeros) is preserved
Comments explain all three cases: normal push, zero SHA, unreachable SHA
userStories.notes:
Files:
- .github/workflows/publish-plugins.yml (detect step, lines 34-57)

Context:
The detect step uses `github.event.before` to find all changed plugins across multi-commit pushes. Currently it checks for zero SHA (first push / force push to new branch) but does NOT check if the SHA is reachable in the repo. A force-push that rewrites history can leave `github.event.before` pointing to a commit that no longer exists, causing `git diff` to fatal with "bad object".

Current code:
```
BEFORE="${{ github.event.before }}"
if [ -z "$BEFORE" ] || echo "$BEFORE" | grep -qE '^0+$'; then
  BEFORE="HEAD~1"
fi
CHANGED_PLUGINS=$(git diff --name-only "$BEFORE" HEAD -- 'plugins/' \
```

Changes:
Add a `git cat-file -e "$BEFORE" 2>/dev/null` check after the zero-SHA check. If it fails (non-zero exit), set BEFORE to HEAD~1. The full guard should be:

```bash
BEFORE="${{ github.event.before }}"
if [ -z "$BEFORE" ] || echo "$BEFORE" | grep -qE '^0+$'; then
  # First push or force push to new branch — no previous SHA
  BEFORE="HEAD~1"
elif ! git cat-file -e "$BEFORE" 2>/dev/null; then
  # SHA exists but is unreachable (force-push rewrote history)
  BEFORE="HEAD~1"
fi
```

Constraints:
- Do NOT change the git diff command itself or the plugin detection logic below it
- Do NOT change the path filters in the `on:` trigger
- Keep the `fetch-depth: 0` in the checkout step (needed for the SHA to be fetchable)

userStories.title: Build plugins with local plugin-tools instead of npm version
userStories.description: In the publish-plugins workflow, after npm install, override @opentabs-dev/plugin-tools with the local monorepo version so that platform fixes to the build tool take effect immediately without waiting for a manual platform publish.
acceptanceCriteria: After `npm install` in each plugin directory, the workflow builds the local plugin-tools and links it into the plugin's node_modules
The `opentabs-plugin build` command inside the plugin uses the monorepo's version of validate-icon.ts, not the npm-published version
The approach does not modify the plugin's package.json or package-lock.json permanently (no dirty git state from the override)
If the local build of plugin-tools fails, the workflow falls back to the npm version with a warning
userStories.notes:
Files:
- .github/workflows/publish-plugins.yml (build step, lines 99-104)

Context:
Plugins are standalone projects that depend on `@opentabs-dev/plugin-tools` from npm. When the workflow runs `npm install` in a plugin directory, it installs the published npm version. If the monorepo has unpublished fixes to plugin-tools (like the decimal-percentage RGB icon fix in validate-icon.ts), the plugin gets built with the old buggy tool.

The monorepo's plugin-tools source is at `platform/plugin-tools/`. It needs to be built (`tsc`) before it can be used. The binary is `dist/cli.js`.

Current code in the publish step:
```bash
echo "Installing dependencies..."
(cd "$DIR" && npm install)

echo "Building..."
(cd "$DIR" && npm run build)
```

Changes:
After `npm install`, build the local plugin-tools and copy it into the plugin's node_modules. The approach:

1. Before the plugin loop, build plugin-tools once:
```bash
echo "Building local plugin-tools..."
(cd platform/plugin-tools && npm run build 2>/dev/null) || echo "::warning::Failed to build local plugin-tools, using npm version"
```

2. Inside the plugin loop, after `npm install`, override with local version:
```bash
echo "Installing dependencies..."
(cd "$DIR" && npm install)

# Override with local plugin-tools to pick up unpublished fixes
if [ -d "platform/plugin-tools/dist" ]; then
  echo "Linking local plugin-tools..."
  rm -rf "$DIR/node_modules/@opentabs-dev/plugin-tools/dist"
  cp -r platform/plugin-tools/dist "$DIR/node_modules/@opentabs-dev/plugin-tools/dist"
fi
```

This copies the built dist/ over the npm-installed version without modifying package.json. The plugin's `opentabs-plugin build` command will use the local version's CLI and validate-icon.ts.

Constraints:
- Do NOT modify the plugin's package.json or package-lock.json
- Do NOT use `npm link` (it creates symlinks that can cause issues with the IIFE bundler)
- The local override must happen AFTER npm install (so the directory structure exists) and BEFORE npm run build
- If platform/plugin-tools/dist doesn't exist (build failed), skip the override silently — the npm version is a safe fallback
- Also need to build the shared package first since plugin-tools depends on it: `(cd platform/shared && npm run build 2>/dev/null)`
